title: SigmaBlockedWalks
tex_block_html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $C \ins J \cup V$ a subset of nodes and $\pi$ a walk in $G$: \[ \pi =\lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp. \] We say that the walk $\pi$ is:

  1. $C$-$\sigma$-open (or $\sigma$-open given $C$) if and only if:
    1. all colliders $v_k$ on $\pi$ are in $\Anc^G(C)$, and:
    2. all blockable non-colliders $v_k$ on $\pi$ are not in $C$.
  2. $C$-$\sigma$-blocked (or $\sigma$-blocked given $C$) if and only if:
    1. there exists a collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ that is not in $\Anc^G(C)$, or:
    2. there exists a blockable non-collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ in $C$.

Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \ins V \cup J$.

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_17 -- "SigmaBlockedWalks".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it compile both standalone (resolving its
% preamble via `main.tex`) and as part of `main.tex` via `\subfile{...}`.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_17
% title: SigmaBlockedWalks
\def\rowref{def\_3\_17}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_17}

\begin{Def}[$\sigma$-blocked walks]\label{def:sigma_blocking}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg}, the family-relationships definition of def \ref{def_3_5}, the collider/non-collider machinery of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, and the blockable/unblockable refinement of def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}. Let $C \subseteq J \cup V$ be a subset of nodes, and let
    \[
        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
    \]
    be a walk in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$:
    \[
        \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr)
        \;\lor\;
        \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr).
    \]

    \emph{Ancestor set of $C$.}
    The \emph{ancestor set} $\Anc^G(C) \subseteq J \cup V$ is the indexed union of the per-vertex ancestor sets of def \ref{def_3_5}, item~iv.:
    \[
        \Anc^G(C) := \bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c).
    \]
    Equivalently, for any $w \in J \cup V$,
    \[
        w \in \Anc^G(C) \quad\iff\quad \exists\, c \in C \text{ such that } w \in \Anc^G(c).
    \]
    In the clauses below we use $\Anc^G(C)$ in this union sense.

    \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-open walk.}
    The walk $\pi$ is said to be \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-open} (also called \emph{$\sigma$-open given $C$}) iff both of the following universal conditions hold:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.\ (equivalently, $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$), then $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$;
        \item for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider} (paragraph ``Blockable non-collider on $\pi$''), then $v_k \notin C$.
    \end{enumerate}
    Both clauses are universally quantified over the position index set $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$; non-collider positions that are \emph{unblockable} (def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}, paragraph ``Unblockable non-collider on $\pi$'') are not constrained by clause~ii., and collider positions are not constrained by clause~ii.\ either (clause~i.\ handles them and clause~ii.\ is vacuous on collider positions since ``blockable non-collider'' carries ``non-collider'' as part of its precondition per def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}).

    \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-blocked walk.}
    The walk $\pi$ is said to be \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-blocked} (also called \emph{$\sigma$-blocked given $C$}) iff at least one of the following existential conditions holds:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ (def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.) and $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$;
        \item there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ (def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}, paragraph ``Blockable non-collider on $\pi$'') and $v_k \in C$.
    \end{enumerate}

    \emph{De Morgan duality of the two classifications.}
    Inspecting the two clause-pairs above, clause~i.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause~i.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' (``there exists a collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$'' is the negation of ``every collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$''), and clause~ii.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause~ii.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' (``there exists a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \in C$'' is the negation of ``every blockable non-collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \notin C$''). Disjoining the two negated clauses thus gives the negation of the conjunction of the two original clauses; consequently
    \[
        \pi \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-blocked} \quad\iff\quad \pi \text{ is not } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},
    \]
    and the two notions are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over walks in $G$.

    \emph{Treatment of the trailing LN remark.}
    The LN source block (\texttt{graphs.tex}, \texttt{\textbackslash label\{def:sigma\_blocking\}}) follows the two definitions above with an embedded \texttt{claimmark} reading ``Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \subseteq J \cup V$.'' Per the row's authoritative \texttt{addition\_to\_the\_LN} clause \texttt{[claim\_type\_mismatch\_vertex\_vs\_walk]}, that remark is \emph{intentionally excluded} from the formalization of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}: it is informal commentary, not part of the definition proper, and it applies the predicate ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' to \emph{vertices} (specifically, to unblockable non-collider occurrences on a walk), whereas the formal definition above only ever introduces ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' as a property of \emph{walks}. No Lean obligation is to be derived from the embedded remark within this definition. Any walk-level reformulation of its underlying content is handled separately as the dedicated claim row \texttt{claim\_3\_21}; the present definition treats the embedded remark as out of scope.
\end{Def}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html:
Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg, the family-relationships definition of def def_3_5, the collider/non-collider machinery of def def:collider_noncollider, and the blockable/unblockable refinement of def def:unblockable_noncollider. Let $C \subseteq J \cup V$ be a subset of nodes, and let \[ \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n) \] be a walk in $G$ (def def:walks, item i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$: \[ \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr) \;\lor\; \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr). \]

Ancestor set of $C$. The ancestor set $\Anc^G(C) \subseteq J \cup V$ is the indexed union of the per-vertex ancestor sets of def def_3_5, item iv.: \[ \Anc^G(C) := \bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c). \] Equivalently, for any $w \in J \cup V$, \[ w \in \Anc^G(C) \quad\iff\quad \exists\, c \in C \text{ such that } w \in \Anc^G(c). \] In the clauses below we use $\Anc^G(C)$ in this union sense.

$C$-$\sigma$-open walk. The walk $\pi$ is said to be $C$-$\sigma$-open (also called $\sigma$-open given $C$) iff both of the following universal conditions hold:

  1. for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:collider_noncollider, item ii.\ (equivalently, $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$), then $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$;
  2. for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:unblockable_noncollider (paragraph ÔÇťBlockable non-collider on $\pi$ÔÇŁ), then $v_k \notin C$.

Both clauses are universally quantified over the position index set $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$; non-collider positions that are unblockable (def def:unblockable_noncollider, paragraph ÔÇťUnblockable non-collider on $\pi$ÔÇŁ) are not constrained by clause ii., and collider positions are not constrained by clause ii.\ either (clause i.\ handles them and clause ii.\ is vacuous on collider positions since ÔÇťblockable non-colliderÔÇŁ carries ÔÇťnon-colliderÔÇŁ as part of its precondition per def def:unblockable_noncollider).

$C$-$\sigma$-blocked walk. The walk $\pi$ is said to be $C$-$\sigma$-blocked (also called $\sigma$-blocked given $C$) iff at least one of the following existential conditions holds:

  1. there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ (def def:collider_noncollider, item ii.) and $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$;
  2. there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ (def def:unblockable_noncollider, paragraph ÔÇťBlockable non-collider on $\pi$ÔÇŁ) and $v_k \in C$.

De Morgan duality of the two classifications. Inspecting the two clause-pairs above, clause i.\ of ÔÇť$C$-$\sigma$-blockedÔÇŁ is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause i.\ of ÔÇť$C$-$\sigma$-openÔÇŁ (ÔÇťthere exists a collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$ÔÇŁ is the negation of ÔÇťevery collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$ÔÇŁ), and clause ii.\ of ÔÇť$C$-$\sigma$-blockedÔÇŁ is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause ii.\ of ÔÇť$C$-$\sigma$-openÔÇŁ (ÔÇťthere exists a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \in C$ÔÇŁ is the negation of ÔÇťevery blockable non-collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \notin C$ÔÇŁ). Disjoining the two negated clauses thus gives the negation of the conjunction of the two original clauses; consequently \[ \pi \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-blocked} \quad\iff\quad \pi \text{ is not } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}, \] and the two notions are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over walks in $G$.

Treatment of the trailing LN remark. The LN source block (graphs.tex, \texttt{\label\{def:sigma\_blocking\}}) follows the two definitions above with an embedded claimmark reading ÔÇťNote that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \subseteq J \cup V$.ÔÇŁ Per the row's authoritative addition_to_the_LN clause [claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk], that remark is intentionally excluded from the formalization of def def:sigma_blocking: it is informal commentary, not part of the definition proper, and it applies the predicate ÔÇť$C$-$\sigma$-openÔÇŁ to vertices (specifically, to unblockable non-collider occurrences on a walk), whereas the formal definition above only ever introduces ÔÇť$C$-$\sigma$-openÔÇŁ as a property of walks. No Lean obligation is to be derived from the embedded remark within this definition. Any walk-level reformulation of its underlying content is handled separately as the dedicated claim row claim_3_21; the present definition treats the embedded remark as out of scope.

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on `IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `IsSigmaBlockedGiven`. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is inherited from the chapter-wide convention: although the two predicates in this row are propositional and do not themselves run any decision procedure, they reach into `G.AncSet C` and `p.IsCollider k` / `p.IsBlockableNonCollider k`, which in turn unfold through `Finset.filter`, `Finset.image`, and option-valued list lookups on `p.vertices` ÔÇö all of which need decidable equality on the element type. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually use it. Same convention as the rest of `Chapter3_GraphTheory`'s files.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability is inherited by `G.AncSet`, `p.IsCollider`, `p.IsBlockableNonCollider` below.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.explanation: A second `variable` line, scoped to the `Walk` sub-namespace, that auto-binds the ambient CDMG `G : CDMG Node` as an implicit argument to every subsequent declaration. Both `IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` take a walk `p : Walk G u v` whose graph parameter must come from somewhere; without this binder each signature would have to spell out `{G : CDMG Node}` explicitly. Lifting it once at the namespace top mirrors the LN's once-at-the-top quantifier "let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG" and keeps the bodies focused on the walk and the conditioning set. `G` is kept implicit because downstream callers reach into it via dot-notation on the walk (`p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C`) ÔÇö Lean recovers `G` from the type of `p`, so the user never types it. The two main predicates also call `G.AncSet C` on the bound `G`, so it must be in scope when their bodies type-check.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Namespace-wide implicit `G : CDMG Node`; recovered from the walk's type via dot-notation at every use site.
variable {G : CDMG Node}

lean_blocks.code:
def IsSigmaOpenGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  (ÔłÇ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôćĺ p.IsCollider k Ôćĺ
      vk Ôłł G.AncSet C) Ôłž
  (ÔłÇ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôćĺ
      p.IsBlockableNonCollider k Ôćĺ vk Ôłë C)
lean_blocks.explanation: This block defines the LN's first classifier ÔÇö a walk $\pi$ is *$C$-$\sigma$-open* ÔÇö as a `Prop`-valued predicate on `(p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node)`. The body is a conjunction of two parallel universals, one per LN clause. Each universal ranges over position indices `k : ÔäĽ` together with the vertex `vk : Node` at that position, pinned down by the hypothesis `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`; this is the standard `getElem?` lookup that returns `none` past the end of the walk, so the LN's index range $k \in \{0, \dots, n\}$ is implicit ÔÇö out-of-range `k` falsify the antecedent and make the implication vacuous. Clause (i) says every collider position has its vertex inside the ancestor set $\Anc^G(C)$, written here as `G.AncSet C` (the indexed-union form from `def_3_5`). Clause (ii) says every *blockable* non-collider position has its vertex outside `C`; using `p.IsBlockableNonCollider k` rather than the broader `p.IsNonCollider k` is load-bearing ÔÇö unblockable non-collider positions are silently outside both clauses, matching the LN's intent. Dot-notation `p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C` reads as the LN's "$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open".
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- ref: def_3_17 (main, LN paragraph "C-¤â-open walk") ÔÇö walk-level `Prop` predicate matching the LN's two-clause universal.
def IsSigmaOpenGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  -- LN clause (i): universal over `(k, vk)` pairs; `getElem?` lookup implicitly bounds `k ÔëĄ p.length`.
  (ÔłÇ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôćĺ p.IsCollider k Ôćĺ
      -- Collider positions must have their vertex inside the indexed-union ancestor set `Anc^G(C)`.
      vk Ôłł G.AncSet C) Ôłž
  -- LN clause (ii): same `(k, vk)` shape, restricted to *blockable* non-colliders only (unblockable positions silently excluded).
  (ÔłÇ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôćĺ
      -- Blockable non-collider positions must have their vertex outside the conditioning set `C`.
      p.IsBlockableNonCollider k Ôćĺ vk Ôłë C)

lean_blocks.code:
def IsSigmaBlockedGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  (Ôłâ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôłž p.IsCollider k Ôłž vk Ôłë G.AncSet C) ÔłĘ
  (Ôłâ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôłž p.IsBlockableNonCollider k Ôłž vk Ôłł C)
lean_blocks.explanation: This block defines the LN's second classifier ÔÇö a walk $\pi$ is *$C$-$\sigma$-blocked* ÔÇö as the positive existential disjunction dual to `IsSigmaOpenGiven`, not as `┬Č p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C`. The body is a `ÔłĘ` of two existentials, each pinning a position index `k : ÔäĽ` and the vertex `vk` at that position via `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`. Disjunct (i) is a collider witness: some position `k` is a collider on `p` whose vertex `vk` escapes `G.AncSet C`. Disjunct (ii) is a blockable-non-collider witness: some position `k` is a blockable non-collider on `p` whose vertex `vk` lies in `C`. Encoding the predicate in this positive form lets downstream proofs build a blocking witness directly as `Or.inl ÔčĘk, vk, h_lookup, h_collider, h_ancÔčę` or `Or.inr ÔčĘk, vk, h_lookup, h_blockable, h_memÔčę`, instead of pushing a double negation through nested universals. The classical De Morgan equivalence `┬Č p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C Ôćö p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C` is left as a downstream lemma rather than a definitional reduction.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- ref: def_3_17 (main, LN paragraph "C-¤â-blocked walk") ÔÇö positive existential disjunction, NOT `┬Č IsSigmaOpenGiven`.
def IsSigmaBlockedGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  -- LN clause (i) dual: a collider witness whose vertex escapes the ancestor set `Anc^G(C)`.
  (Ôłâ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node),
      -- Triple-conjunct destructures cleanly as `ÔčĘh_lookup, h_collider, h_ancÔčę` at the use site.
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôłž p.IsCollider k Ôłž vk Ôłë G.AncSet C) ÔłĘ
  -- LN clause (ii) dual: a *blockable* non-collider witness whose vertex lies in `C` (unblockable positions cannot witness).
  (Ôłâ (k : ÔäĽ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk Ôłž p.IsBlockableNonCollider k Ôłž vk Ôłł C)

addition_to_the_LN: [claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk] The trailing claim asserting that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open is excluded from the formalization. It is informal commentary, not part of Definition~\ref{def:sigma_blocking}, and no Lean obligation should be derived from it.
design_choices:
**Walk-level `Prop` predicates over `(p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node)`.** The LN classifies *walks* relative to a conditioning set, so `Walk.IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `Walk.IsSigmaBlockedGiven` take the walk and the set directly and dot-notation `p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C` reads as "$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open". No vertex-level $\sigma$-open predicate is introduced: the trailing LN remark that "unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open" applies the walk-level predicate to vertices, which the addition `[claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk]` records as a type mismatch and excludes from this row; any walk-level reformulation is deferred to `claim_3_21`.

**Reuse `p.IsCollider` and `p.IsBlockableNonCollider` from `def_3_15` and `def_3_16`, coupled to vertices via `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`.** Using the existing position predicates as antecedents keeps the LN's "for every collider on $\pi$" scope visible at the type level instead of re-spelling the arrowhead-count case-split. The option-valued lookup pins `vk` to the walk and implicitly bounds $k \le n$ ÔÇö out-of-range lookups return `none`, so universal antecedents are vacuous and existential witnesses cannot form past the end, matching the LN's scoping to $\{0, \dots, n\}$ without an explicit upper-bound hypothesis.

**`p.IsBlockableNonCollider k`, not `p.IsNonCollider k`.** Clause (ii) restricts to *blockable* non-colliders specifically; unblockable positions are silently outside both classifications. The broader `IsNonCollider` would over-fire on the open side (forcing unblockable positions outside $C$) and under-fire on the blocked side (admitting unblockable positions in $C$ as blocking witnesses), neither of which the LN says. This is the predicate-level realisation of the LN's intent and the structural counterpart of `[claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk]`.

**Positive existential disjunction for `IsSigmaBlockedGiven`, not `┬Č IsSigmaOpenGiven`.** The LN spells blockedness as $\exists \dots \notin \Anc^G(C) \;\lor\; \exists \dots \in C$, the De Morgan dual of the open definition. Encoding it directly in that positive form lets downstream proofs build a blocking witness as an immediate `Or.inl ÔčĘk, vk, h_lookup, h_collider, h_ancÔčę` or `Or.inr ÔčĘk, vk, h_lookup, h_blockable, h_memÔčę` term, instead of pushing a double negation through nested universals and conjunctions. The classical equivalence `┬Č p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C Ôćö p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C` is a downstream De Morgan lemma, not a definitional reduction.

**Parallel two-clause shape on both predicates.** Matching the LN's bullet list lets `IsSigmaOpenGiven` destructure as `ÔčĘh_collider, h_blockableÔčę` and keeps the existential witnesses of `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` symmetric to those clauses, so any future duality lemma aligns witness-to-witness. A single universal over a sum-typed combined predicate would force every downstream proof to recover the bullet-list shape by hand.

**Reuse `G.AncSet` from `def_3_5` and namespace-level `variable` binders.** `CDMG.AncSet` already encodes the indexed-union ancestor set $\bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c)$; out-of-graph elements of $C$ contribute `G.Anc c = Ôłů` via the `w Ôłł G` guard inside `Anc`, so $C \subseteq J \cup V$ is not needed at the def site. `{Node : Type*}`, `[DecidableEq Node]`, and `{G : CDMG Node}` are lifted at the `CDMG` and inner `Walk` namespaces, matching the chapter convention and the LN's once-at-the-top "Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$" quantifier. Decidability is inherited because `G.AncSet` and the position predicates unfold through `Finset.filter`, `Finset.image`, and `p.vertices` lookups; `G` stays implicit because callers recover it from the walk's type via dot-notation.
